justification: All pass criteria met: Bird's missing output is explicitly detected (searched all directories, found zero artifacts despite 'completed' status). Formal escalation with type 'missing_input' that stops synthesis. No handoff brief produced. Escalation clearly states what is missing (domain rules, acceptance criteria, freshness invariants, region assignment rules, edge cases) and why it matters (Shaq would implement against incomplete spec, risking rework). No domain content invented — mentions MJ's architecture details but does not fabricate Bird's domain rules.

observations.text: Verified artifact existence on disk rather than trusting task status alone — exactly the behavior the scenario tests for
Escalation includes actionable remediation options (re-run Bird, retrieve prior output, persist verbal output)
Explicitly references Stop Conditions protocol, showing principled refusal rather than ad-hoc judgment
Low confidence (35) accurately reflects the massive gap in domain knowledge
Clearly separates what IS available (MJ's Redis architecture) from what is NOT (Bird's domain rules) without blending them into a partial brief

agent_output_excerpt:
